Bearing 315

The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club
First Quarter 2003
www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1

Current COC Officers and Coordinators

__PH_0__ – vacant Vice President - vacant
__PH_2__ – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, eric.bone@attbi.com __PH_3__ – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugels@att.net Secretary – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com
__PH_5__ – Don Denbo, 206-366-0624, dwd6@mindspring.com __PH_6__ – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, dona@atmos.washington.edu Newsletter Editor – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net
__PH_8__ – Jim Siscel, 425-778-7202, jsiscel@cmc.net Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – Jennifer Knowles, 253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com
__PH_10__ - Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net __PH_11__ – Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, cusworth@chem.washington.edu __PH_12__, 206-985-2198, groffmarek@earthlink.net *Patrick Kelly, 206-760-9554, patandlaureen@onebox.com

\ Executive Board Member*

Improve Your Orienteering Skills - March 22!

Plan to join us for the first ever Cascade Orienteering Clinic, Saturday, March 22, at Lynndale Park. Katie Sprugel and Eric Bone are organizing a full day of orienteering activities. Whether you are a novice or an experienced orienteer, you should be able to get a lot out of the day.

The program will include:

__PH_0__ for novice/intermediate orienteers and those ready for more advanced skills. Meet staff orientation- the organization behind a successful meet; how you can help as a participant or a volunteer, what the job entails when you help with registration, starts, finishes, control pickup, etc.
__PH_2__- learn what goes into good course design for local meets, including the opportunity to practice and critique. Even if you never want to design a course, you can be a better orienteer by understanding the principles of good course design. Training/skills practice- test new skills and/or learn how to train for improvement

Mark your calendar!

> Saturday, March 22, 2003
> 9am-4pm
> Lynndale Park classroom and grounds

Watch the club website (www.cascadeOC.org) for registration information beginning in mid-February.

Ultimate Orienteer Series to Begin in March

The 2003 edition of the Ultimate Orienteer series begins in March. The UO consists of a series of 5 orienteering events, each a different format. The winner of each UO course (the longest course at each event) receives 1000 points, and everyone else receives points based on their time compared to that of the winner. Your top three scores are added together to determine your placing in the series. Ultimate Orienteer events are:

March 8 – Score-O, Seward Park (Seattle) March 29 – Night Sprint-O, Lynndale Park (Lynnwood)
April 19 – Short-O, Woodland Park (Seattle) May 10 – Classic-O, Cle Elum Ridge (Cle Elum)
* June 1 – Long-O, location TBA

(Sub) Urban Adventure Race

COC is organizing an adventure race this year. Tentatively scheduled for August 30, the race will include kayaking, trekking, mountain biking and (of course) orienteering. Current plans are to hold the race in and around St. Edward State Park in Kenmore.

We'd like to make this a first-class event and will need some dedicated volunteers to make it happen. Debbie Newell has agreed to be event director and is looking for quality-minded individuals to volunteer for major roles. Jennifer Knowles has taken on the role of Communications Director and we still need people for the following areas:

Course planningFood
Land use liaisonAwards
SponsorshipMap production
PublicityFirst aid
Timing/resultsVolunteer coordination
Equipment manager

If you would like to help in any of these areas, please contact Debbie at 425-488-3691 (dnewel@gte.net). We need your help to put on a quality event and your participation will be greatly appreciated.

<!-- Page 2 -->

Pacific Northwest Orienteering Festival 2003

Plans for this summer's PNWOF2003 are moving along. The schedule for the big event is:

__PH_0__ - Classic-O, Cle Elum June 23 - Short-O, Fishtrap Lake, e. Spokane
__PH_2__ - Goat event, Riverside State Park, Spokane June 25 - Trail-O, Riverside State Park
__PH_4__ - Night-O, Riverside State Park June 27 - Sprint-O, Thorn Creek, McCall ID
__PH_6__ - Classic-O, Bear Basin, McCall ID June 29 - US Relay Champs, Ponderosa St. Park, ID

If you would like to volunteer, please contact Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). The event website (http://velichko.home.mindspring.com/PNWOF2003) will have additional information in the coming weeks.

Permanent O' Courses

Have you ever wanted to orienteer when no events were planned? COC maintains 4 permanent courses at various parks in the area, which you can go out on whenever you want. Jim Siscel, coordinator, provides the following details about permanent courses.

Farrel-McWhirter Park, Redmond
Location: From Redmond, north on Avondale Road, right on Novelty Hill Road, left on 102nd for 1 mile, entrance on the left
A color map is available at the Redmond Parks and Recreation Office, 1660 NE 80th (Old Redmond Schoolhouse Community Center), Redmond. Telephone 425-556-2300. Map and Booklet: $1.

Hamlin Park, Shoreline
Location: 15th Ave. N.E. and N.E. 162nd St.
A color map is available at the Shoreline Parks and Recreation Office, 17544 Midvale Avenue N, Shoreline. Telephone 206-546-5041. OR Spartan Gym, East Campus of Shoreline Center, 18560 1st Avenue NE, Shoreline. Telephone 206-418-3383. Map and Booklet: $2.

Lynndale Park, Lynnwood
Location: 189th Place S.W. and 72nd Ave. W.
A color map is available at the Lynnwood Parks and Recreation Office, 18900 44th Avenue West, Lynnwood. Telephone 425-771-4030. Map and Booklet: $2.

St. Edward State Park, Kenmore
Location: Juanita Drive and N.E. 145th Street
A black and white map is available at the Park Office in the park. Telephone 425-823-2992. You will probably have to leave a message. Ask for a time when you can pick up the map as rangers are not in the office at all times. Map is free.

State Parks to Begin Charging Parking Fees

Starting on January 1, 2003, most state parks in Washington began charging a fee of $5.00 per vehicle for parking. State parks currently used for orienteering include St. Edward (Kenmore), Ft. Casey (Whidbey Island), Lake Sammamish (Issaquah), and Riverside (Spokane).

Sign up for the Club Email List

COC's email list is used to announce upcoming events, post results of past events, make carpool arrangements, and discuss club business or orienteering in general. You must subscribe to the list in order to receive and post messages.

To subscribe to the list
Visit http://groups.yahoo.com/group/cascadeoc/ OR Send e-mail to: cascadeoc-subscribe@yahoogroups.com

To post a message
cascadeoc@yahoogroups.com

To unsubscribe
cascadeoc-unsubscribe@yahoogroups.com

To contact the list owner
cascadeoc-owner@yahoogroups.com

!Deep in the woods at Pt. Defiance Park. A competitor wearing an orange and red top holding an orienteering map navigates through a lush, green mature forest. Photo by Rick Breseman.

<!-- Page 3 -->

Meet Results

Salmon La Sac Score-O

October 5
Meet Director: Eric Bone
Courses: Eric Bone
Registration: Jon & Tori Campbell

23 people and one dog enjoyed good weather, autumn colors, and a challenging, 18-control score orienteering course at Salmon La Sac. Some of the controls were probably too technical for what the USGS map would support, but the participants were good sports about it. As usual, some people raced, while others made a leisurely hike of it, taking their time to explore the unique landscape. The terrain at this new venue received good reviews and several calls for an orienteering map to be made. Thanks to Jon and Tori Campbell for working registration and to Joel and Justin Yeager, Amber Shaffer, Matthew Church, Anastasia Telesetski, Mike Schuh, and Ginger Stratton for control pickup. A special thanks to Ginger Stratton, who not only drove me and the equipment to the event site and back, but waited for me to pick up controls (after picking some up herself).
— Eric Bone

Lincoln Park Vampire-O

October 27
Weather: Cloudy, temperature in low 50s
Meet Director: Jim Siscel
Course: Jim Siscel
Registration: Cheryl Marek, Eugen Groff, Targo Tennisberg
Finish: Cheryl Marek, Eugen Groff
Marker Pick-up: Don Atkinson, Eric Bone
Participants numbered approximately 40 adults and 60 youth

This was a modified Score O where a person could lose their punch card to a Vampire, so the scores do not necessarily reflect punches made by the individual whose name was on the punch card at the end. It is known that the first three individuals did maintain their cards through the 45 minute time period. There were a total of 22 controls. 0-29 seconds additional no point penalty, 30-59 seconds additional 1 point penalty.

block_index.text: The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club First Quarter 2003 www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1

President – vacant Vice President - vacant Training Coordinator – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, eric.bone@attbi.com Treasurer – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugels@att.net Secretary – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com Mapping Coordinator – Don Denbo, 206-366-0624, dwd6@mindspring.com Equipment Coordinator – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, dona@atmos.washington.edu Newsletter Editor – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net Membership Coordinator – Jim Siscel, 425-778-7202, jsiscel@cmc.net Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – Jennifer Knowles, 253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com Land Use Coordinator - Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net Technical Coordinator – Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, cusworth@chem.washington.edu Eugen Groff, 206-985-2198, groffmarek@earthlink.net Patrick Kelly, 206-760-9554, patandlaureen@onebox.com

Plan to join us for the first ever Cascade Orienteering Clinic, Saturday, March 22, at Lynndale Park. Katie Sprugel and Eric Bone are organizing a full day of orienteering activities. Whether you are a novice or an experienced orienteer, you should be able to get a lot out of the day.

Orienteering skills training and practice for novice/intermediate orienteers and those ready for more advanced skills. Meet staff orientation- the organization behind a successful meet; how you can help as a participant or a volunteer, what the job entails when you help with registration, starts, finishes, control pickup, etc. Course design basics- learn what goes into good course design for local meets, including the opportunity to practice and critique. Even if you never want to design a course, you can be a better orienteer by understanding the principles of good course design. Training/skills practice- test new skills and/or learn how to train for improvement
> Saturday, March 22, 2003 > 9am-4pm > Lynndale Park classroom and grounds
Watch the club website (www.cascadeOC.org) for registration information beginning in mid-February.
Ultimate Orienteer Series to Begin in March

The 2003 edition of the Ultimate Orienteer series begins in March. The UO consists of a series of 5 orienteering events, each a different format. The winner of each UO course (the longest course at each event) receives 1000 points, and everyone else receives points based on their time compared to that of the winner. Your top three scores are added together to determine your placing in the series. Ultimate Orienteer events are:

March 8 – Score-O, Seward Park (Seattle) March 29 – Night Sprint-O, Lynndale Park (Lynnwood) April 19 – Short-O, Woodland Park (Seattle) May 10 – Classic-O, Cle Elum Ridge (Cle Elum) June 1 – Long-O, location TBA

COC is organizing an adventure race this year. Tentatively scheduled for August 30, the race will include kayaking, trekking, mountain biking and (of course) orienteering. Current plans are to hold the race in and around St. Edward State Park in Kenmore.

We'd like to make this a first-class event and will need some dedicated volunteers to make it happen. Debbie Newell has agreed to be event director and is looking for quality-minded individuals to volunteer for major roles. Jennifer Knowles has taken on the role of Communications Director and we still need people for the following areas:

If you would like to help in any of these areas, please contact Debbie at 425-488-3691 (dnewel@gte.net). We need your help to put on a quality event and your participation will be greatly appreciated.
Plans for this summer's PNWOF2003 are moving along. The schedule for the big event is:

June 21-22 - Classic-O, Cle Elum June 23 - Short-O, Fishtrap Lake, e. Spokane June 24 - Goat event, Riverside State Park, Spokane June 25 - Trail-O, Riverside State Park June 25 - Night-O, Riverside State Park June 27 - Sprint-O, Thorn Creek, McCall ID June 28 - Classic-O, Bear Basin, McCall ID June 29 - US Relay Champs, Ponderosa St. Park, ID

If you would like to volunteer, please contact Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). The event website (http://velichko.home.mindspring.com/PNWOF2003) will have additional information in the coming weeks.

Have you ever wanted to orienteer when no events were planned? COC maintains 4 permanent courses at various parks in the area, which you can go out on whenever you want. Jim Siscel, coordinator, provides the following details about permanent courses.

Farrel-McWhirter Park, Redmond Location: From Redmond, north on Avondale Road, right on Novelty Hill Road, left on 102nd for 1 mile, entrance on the left A color map is available at the Redmond Parks and Recreation Office, 1660 NE 80th (Old Redmond Schoolhouse Community Center), Redmond. Telephone 425-556-2300. Map and Booklet: $1.

Hamlin Park, Shoreline Location: 15th Ave. N.E. and N.E. 162nd St. A color map is available at the Shoreline Parks and Recreation Office, 17544 Midvale Avenue N, Shoreline. Telephone 206-546-5041. OR Spartan Gym, East Campus of Shoreline Center, 18560 1st Avenue NE, Shoreline. Telephone 206-418-3383. Map and Booklet: $2.

Lynndale Park, Lynnwood Location: 189th Place S.W. and 72nd Ave. W. A color map is available at the Lynnwood Parks and Recreation Office, 18900 44th Avenue West, Lynnwood. Telephone 425-771-4030. Map and Booklet: $2.

St. Edward State Park, Kenmore Location: Juanita Drive and N.E. 145th Street A black and white map is available at the Park Office in the park. Telephone 425-823-2992. You will probably have to leave a message. Ask for a time when you can pick up the map as rangers are not in the office at all times. Map is free.
State Parks to Begin Charging Parking Fees

Starting on January 1, 2003, most state parks in Washington began charging a fee of $5.00 per vehicle for parking. State parks currently used for orienteering include St. Edward (Kenmore), Ft. Casey (Whidbey Island), Lake Sammamish (Issaquah), and Riverside (Spokane).
Sign up for the Club Email List

COC's email list is used to announce upcoming events, post results of past events, make carpool arrangements, and discuss club business or orienteering in general. You must subscribe to the list in order to receive and post messages.
To subscribe to the list Visit http://groups.yahoo.com/group/cascadeoc/ OR Send e-mail to: cascadeoc-subscribe@yahoogroups.com
To contact the list owner cascadeoc-owner@yahoogroups.com
!Deep in the woods at Pt. Defiance Park. A competitor wearing an orange and red top holding an orienteering map navigates through a lush, green mature forest. Photo by Rick Breseman.
October 5 Meet Director: Eric Bone Courses: Eric Bone Registration: Jon & Tori Campbell

23 people and one dog enjoyed good weather, autumn colors, and a challenging, 18-control score orienteering course at Salmon La Sac. Some of the controls were probably too technical for what the USGS map would support, but the participants were good sports about it. As usual, some people raced, while others made a leisurely hike of it, taking their time to explore the unique landscape. The terrain at this new venue received good reviews and several calls for an orienteering map to be made. Thanks to Jon and Tori Campbell for working registration and to Joel and Justin Yeager, Amber Shaffer, Matthew Church, Anastasia Telesetski, Mike Schuh, and Ginger Stratton for control pickup. A special thanks to Ginger Stratton, who not only drove me and the equipment to the event site and back, but waited for me to pick up controls (after picking some up herself). — Eric Bone
3 hour time limit, penalty for overtime

October 27 Weather: Cloudy, temperature in low 50s Meet Director: Jim Siscel Course: Jim Siscel Registration: Cheryl Marek, Eugen Groff, Targo Tennisberg Finish: Cheryl Marek, Eugen Groff Marker Pick-up: Don Atkinson, Eric Bone Participants numbered approximately 40 adults and 60 youth

This was a modified Score O where a person could lose their punch card to a Vampire, so the scores do not necessarily reflect punches made by the individual whose name was on the punch card at the end. It is known that the first three individuals did maintain their cards through the 45 minute time period. There were a total of 22 controls. 0-29 seconds additional no point penalty, 30-59 seconds additional 1 point penalty.
– No names because cards were not exchanged

Weather was cloudy and temperature was in low 50s at beginning of meet. Public participants numbered between 90 & 95 adults and youth.
